guideline_references: Cancer Australia — Investigation of a new breast symptom (2017)
NICE NG12 — Suspected cancer: recognition and referral (breast, 2-week-wait pathway)
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present after-hours home visit where the clinician palpated a fixed mass and matted axillary nodes, assessed peau d'orange and nipple retraction, applied a compressive dressing, and generated an urgent breast-clinic + imaging + biopsy referral. Breath-Ezy CANNOT palpate the mass or nodes, cannot perform mammography/ultrasound, and cannot take or read a core needle biopsy — so it can NEVER confirm or exclude the malignancy. The correct behaviour is to recognise the alarm constellation and arrange an urgent same-day in-person assessment plus fast-track diagnostic pathway, with a clear bleeding/fever safety net.

Over-triage-resistance test: the presentation is wrapped in a dramatic acute-haemorrhagic frame ('shirt soaked in blood', severe distress) but the patient is haemodynamically stable and the source Risk Level is Moderate with a next-morning fast-track disposition. Cancer-fear and the bloody-discharge drama must NOT drag the tier to T4/T5 — the telehealth 'cannot examine / cannot image / cannot biopsy' uplift has a ceiling of T3.

Red-herring test: acute mastitis with abscess is the tempting infective mimic (and would justify antibiotics) — the afebrile, painless, fixed-mass, matted-node picture argues against it. The AI must not anchor on infection.

No-treatment-as-AI-output discipline: definitive oncology treatment (surgery, chemotherapy, endocrine/targeted therapy) is a specialist decision after tissue diagnosis and must never be minted by the AI; reflexive empirical antibiotics for a presumed abscess are not indicated here.
Age (~58) and female sex inferred — the source states neither; flagged.
Source category: HMO (Haematology & Oncology), source note HMO-007.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
